notes: Three vibe lifetimes back-to-back — variant 1 (clean exit via Ctrl+D/`/exit`) → variant 2 (SIGKILL mid-idle) → variant 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn). vibe is single-birth (stable session_<ts>_<hash> id from launch), so each variant's birth is one session_birth anchored to the prior variant's process_exited; each has an explicit turn_start so the matcher finds the post-turn ready, not the handoff ready. Session-end signal is process_exited (transcripts persist on disk). No observation vector — lifecycle scenario, and per-turn cost is not emitted by the vibe adapter.
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Session end (3 variants); mirrors claudecode's session-end phase chain

text: Variant 1: first user prompt drives ready → working
Variant 1: turn completes back to ready
Variant 1: clean exit — process_exited fires AFTER state returned to ready (proves 'State is ready at exit')
Variant 2: new session appears in ready after variant 1 finishes
Variant 2: user prompt drives ready → working
Variant 2: turn completes back to ready (idle window begins)

Variant 2: SIGKILL mid-idle — process_exited fires while session is in ready; no transition to working/waiting between v2_turn_done and process_exited (satisfies 'state never entered working post-exit')
Variant 3: new session appears in ready after variant 2 finishes
Variant 3: user prompt drives ready → working
Variant 3: SIGKILL mid-turn — process_exited fires while session is in working; live sweep-to-ready may not appear in the 8s tail (per recipe notes)
